episode_name: Palace of Justice seizure and retaking
theater_description: The Palace of Justice building on Plaza de Bolívar in Bogotá, with co-located hostages/civilians, M-19 guerrillas, and state retaking forces during a 28-hour siege/retaking cycle.
geometry_method_note: Exposure is framed at the building/site level rather than city-wide. Civilians are the hostages and other occupants inside the building; combatants are the M-19 attackers plus the state retaking force actually committed to the site.
military_area_note: Military exposure combines 35 M-19 attackers with about 1,000 state troops. An unquantified police special command is mentioned in narrative sources but is not added to the denominator.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure uses more than 300 hostages/occupants physically inside the Palace. No Bogotá-wide civilian population is used.
split_reason: A single site-bounded episode gives the strongest available alignment between direct deaths and theater-specific denominator construction.
quality_note: Publishable formal DR is possible here because civilians and combatants were co-located in a bounded site, but the final death split is source-contingent and disappeared/unidentified cases remain contested.
